升.origin: Picture a ladle scooping up grain and rising — 升 means to rise or go up.

钟.origin: The metal radical 钅 plus 中 (center) — a metal bell at the center of town that rings the hour: a clock.

厅.origin: The cliff-like roof 厂 shelters 丁 — a big open room or hall where people gather.

sentence.en: We watch TV in the living room.

另.origin: A mouth 口 over strength 力 — point and say 'that other one!' 另 means another.

宝.origin: Jade 玉 kept safe under a house roof 宀 — your most precious treasure.

英.origin: Grass 艹 on top growing tall and beautiful — 英 means a flower's petal, and also heroic.

雄.origin: The short-tailed bird 隹 on the right — 雄 is the male bird, the strong hero.

顺.origin: A flowing river 川 beside a head 页 — go smoothly with the current. 顺 means smooth.

充.origin: Picture pouring more in until it brims over the top — 充 means to fill up.

秘.origin: Grain 禾 beside 必 (sound) — hidden away like seeds in the ground. 秘 means secret.

永.origin: Picture a long river flowing on and on without end — 永 means forever.

歇.origin: A wide-open yawn 欠 on the right — when you yawn, it's time to 歇, to rest.

sentence.en: Let's sit down and rest a while.

吟.origin: A mouth 口 beside 今 (sound) — the mouth softly sings or hums a poem. 吟 means to chant.

sentence.en: He is softly reciting a poem.

慈.origin: A heart 心 at the bottom full of warmth — 慈 means kind and loving, like a caring grandma.

线.origin: The silk-thread radical 纟 on the left — a thin thread or line you can pull. 线 means line.

临.origin: Picture leaning down close over something to look at it — 临 means to come near, to face.

sentence.en: We changed the plan at the last minute.

缝.origin: The silk-thread radical 纟 beside 逢 (sound) — pull thread to join cloth. 缝 means to sew.

恐.origin: A heart 心 at the bottom pounding hard — 恐 is the trembly feeling of fear.

sentence.en: I love looking at dinosaurs the most.

迟.origin: The walking road 辶 wrapping a foot — taking slow steps, arriving late. 迟 means late.

归.origin: Simplified from 歸 — picture coming back home where you belong. 归 means to return.

寸.origin: A hand with a tiny dot marking the pulse, about an inch up the wrist — 寸 means inch.

sentence.en: This cloth is only one inch long.

无.origin: Picture empty hands held up with nothing in them — 无 means none, not having.

sentence.en: There are no clouds in the sky.

改.origin: A hand with a stick 攵 helping a child 己 do better — 改 means to change or fix.

衰.origin: A person in a robe 衣 drooping with a mark inside — 衰 means weak and worn out.

sentence.en: The old man's body is weak.

童.origin: A child standing 立 in the village 里 — 童 means child.

何.origin: A person 亻 beside 可 (sound) asking with a shrug — 何 means what or why.

冲.origin: Two ice drops 冫 plus 中 (center) — water rushing straight through the middle. 冲 means to rush, to rinse.

sentence.en: He rushed over and hugged me.

称.origin: Grain 禾 on the left being weighed and named — 称 means to weigh or to call something.

word.en: to address, a form of address

官.origin: Under a big roof 宀 sits someone in charge — 官 means an official.

重.origin: A heavy load carried a thousand 千 miles 里 — 重 means heavy.

秤.origin: A flat, level 平 tool for weighing grain 禾 — 秤 means a scale or balance.

sentence.en: Weigh the apples on the scale.

摇.origin: A hand 扌 on the left doing the shaking — 摇 means to rock or shake.

sentence.en: The puppy is wagging its tail.

赶.origin: The walking-fast 走 radical means running — 赶 means to chase or hurry after.

沿.origin: Water 氵 on the left flowing along its banks — 沿 means to follow along.

沉.origin: Too much 冗 water 氵 pulls things down — 沉 means to sink.

sentence.en: The stone sank into the water.

赞.origin: The shell 贝 at the bottom meant money long ago — give support and praise. 赞 means to praise.

镜.origin: The metal radical 钅 plus 竟 (sound) — polished metal you see yourself in. 镜 means mirror.

参.origin: Picture joining a group and adding your part — 参 means to take part, to join in.

sentence.en: I want to take part in the contest.

任.origin: A person 亻 beside 壬 (sound) you can lean on — 任 means to trust or a duty.

而.origin: Originally a drawing of a beard hanging down; today 而 is a little word meaning 'and' or 'but'.
